title: Business and related research professionals
description: Analyze and coordinate the ongoing logistical functions of a firm or organization. Responsible for the entire life cycle of a product, including acquisition, distribution, internal allocation, delivery, and final disposal of resources. Design or analyze operational solutions for projects such as transportation optimization, network modeling, process and methods analysis, cost containment, capacity enhancement, routing and shipment optimization, or information management. Analyze product delivery or supply chain processes to identify or recommend changes. May manage route activity including invoicing, electronic bills, and shipment tracing. (Based on 75 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Review and evaluate notes taken about news events to isolate pertinent facts and details.
Investigate breaking news developments, such as disasters, crimes, or human-interest stories.
Write online blog entries that address news developments or offer additional information, opinions, or commentary on news events.
Communicate with readers, viewers, advertisers, or the general public via mail, email, or telephone.
Conduct taped or filmed interviews or narratives, and arrange interviews with people who can provide information about a story.
Gather information and develop perspectives about news subjects through research, interviews, observation, and experience.
Report news stories for publication or broadcast, describing the background and details of events, and verify facts, dates, and statistics.
Measure and assess customer and employee satisfaction, and collect and analyze data on customer demographics, preferences, needs, and buying habits.
Assess and analyze logistics data involving customer service, forecasting, procurement, manufacturing, inventory, transportation, or warehousing.
Identify opportunities for inventory reductions and determine feasibility of designing new facilities or modifying existing facilities.
Review contractual commitments, customer specifications, or related information to determine logistics or support requirements.
Contact carriers for rates or schedules, and coordinate with service providers such as ocean carriers or trucking companies.
Analyze logistics data and interpret metrics including on-time delivery rates, order fulfillment rates, and inventory turns.
Develop logistic metrics, internal analysis tools, or key performance indicators to identify cost-reduction or process-improvement opportunities.
Evaluate the use of technologies like GPS, RFID, or route navigation software to improve transportation efficiency.
Prepare logistic strategies, conceptual designs for production facilities, and develop or document procedures to minimize carbon output.
Conduct environmental audits related to logistics activities and assess the environmental impact or energy efficiency of logistics operations.
Maintain records and databases of logistics information, ensuring compliance with corporate policies and procedures.
Review procedures such as distribution or inventory management to ensure maximum efficiency or minimum cost.
Prepare reports on logistics performance measures and provide ongoing analyses of transportation costs or delivery processes.
Manage systems to ensure pricing structures reflect logistics costing and monitor inventory transactions at warehouse facilities.
Analyze production costs and report findings.
Manage the logistical aspects of product life cycles, coordinating resources and minimizing obsolescence.
Support the development of training materials and technical manuals for logistics operations.
Direct and support the compilation and analysis of technical source data necessary for the development of products.
Plan, organize, and execute logistics support activities, including maintenance planning and repair analysis.
Collaborate with other departments to meet customer requirements and minimize negative impacts on a business.
Prepare reports documenting project plans, progress, and results.
Develop proposals with complete documentation for cost estimates.
Analyze and report logistics performance with customers against targets and service agreements.
Examine and revise design alternatives to assess their impacts on projects.
Plan study of work problems and procedures, such as organizational change, communications, information flow, and cost analysis.
Document findings of study and prepare recommendations for implementation of new systems or procedures.
Compile and evaluate statistical data to determine and maintain quality and reliability of products.
Perform validations or transfers of analytical methods in accordance with applicable policies or guidelines.
Identify quality problems and recommend solutions based on analysis and observations.
Write technical reports or documentation, such as deviation reports and testing protocols.
Document software defects, using a bug tracking system, and report defects to software developers.
Investigate customer problems referred by technical support and provide feedback and recommendations.
Collaborate with field staff or customers to evaluate or diagnose problems and recommend possible solutions.
Review software documentation to ensure completeness, accuracy, and compliance.
Conduct research to obtain factual information and authentic detail, using sources such as newspaper accounts, diaries, and interviews.
Plan project arrangements or outlines, and organize material accordingly.

Prepare works in appropriate format for publication, and send them to publishers or producers.
Document methods used and write technical reports containing information collected.
Write articles, bulletins, sales letters, speeches, and other related informative, marketing and promotional material.
